chunks.page_summary: This page provides a getting started guide for setting up Rheonics products, detailing necessary hardware, software, and configuration steps for the Rheonics Control Panel.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Getting Started
Modified on: Tue, 12 Oct, 2021 at 4:40 AM

1. Getting Things Ready
1.1. Rheonics SRV, SRD, DVP or DVM w/ Firmware V03.20/0 or higher
1.2. Allen Bradley Micro820 2080-LC20-20QBB
1.3. Rockwell Automation CCW Version 12.00.00 (includes RSLinx Classic Lite 4.11.00 CPR 9 SR 11.0) and “EDS Hardware Installation tool 26.0.27.0”)
1.4. Windows 10 64 bit
1.5. RheonicsSRV.eds, RheonicsSRD.eds, RheonicsDVP.eds or RheonicsDVM.eds file
1.6. RheonicsSRVEval_micro820_4A.ccwarc and RheonicsSRVEval_micro820_4B.ccwarc file
1.7. Ethernet Switch

2. Rheonics Control Panel Setup
2.1. Connect Rheonics sensor, PLC, and PC (with RHEONICS CONTROL PANEL software installed) with an Ethernet Cable. We recommend using an Ethernet Switch.
2.2. Open RHEONICS CONTROL PANEL software and configure the RHEONICS CONTROL PANEL to use DHCP. Make sure it has a valid IP address. Figure 1 shows a standard configuration that can be used for the correct performance of the system.
2.2.1. Go to the “Comm Configure tab” in the RHEONICS CONTROL PANEL; in the Ethernet section input all the parameters.
2.2.2. Click on the “Update SMET” button to upload the new settings into the SMET
key_facts: Rheonics SRV, SRD, DVP, or DVM products require firmware V03.20/0 or higher.
The setup requires an Allen Bradley Micro820 2080-LC20-20QBB.
Required software includes Rockwell Automation CCW Version 12.00.00, RSLinx Classic Lite 4.11.00 CPR 9 SR 11.0, and EDS Hardware Installation tool 26.0.27.0.
A Windows 10 64-bit operating system is required.
Specific EDS files (RheonicsSRV.eds, RheonicsSRD.eds, RheonicsDVP.eds, RheonicsDVM.eds) are needed for Rheonics sensors.
Specific CCWARC files (RheonicsSRVEval_micro820_4A.ccwarc and RheonicsSRVEval_micro820_4B.ccwarc) are mentioned.
An Ethernet Switch is required for system connectivity.
The Rheonics Control Panel software must be configured to use DHCP and have a valid IP address for optimal system performance.
Network configuration parameters for the Rheonics Control Panel are entered in the 'Comm Configure tab' within the Ethernet section.
New settings are uploaded to the SMET device by clicking the 'Update SMET' button.
warnings_notes: We recommend using an Ethernet Switch for connecting the Rheonics sensor, PLC, and PC.
Make sure the RHEONICS CONTROL PANEL has a valid IP address for the correct performance of the system.

key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software is version 4.3.0.2.
Ethernet IP configuration can be set using either DHCP or a Static IP address.
To enable a Static IP address, the DHCP setting in the Ethernet section must be set to 'False', which disables the DHCP function.
When configuring a Static IP, all Ethernet/IP parameters must be manually filled with valid values.
The control panel allows configuration of Modbus RTU (RS-485) settings, including Modbus Address, Baud Rate, and Parity.
The LCD display for SME-TRD devices can be configured to show process parameters like Viscosity, Density, and Temperature with specified display resolutions.
Operational history data, including Real-Time Clock (RTC) time, supply voltage, hardware version, SME temperature, and maximum sensor/SME temperature exposures, is recorded and displayed.
The device supports Bluetooth LTE 4.0 connectivity, identified by MAC address 70:B3:D5:D2:02:7B.
The Modbus Baud Rate and Parity settings are configurable and shown with different values in the DHCP (19200, EVEN) and Static IP (38400, ODD) examples.
diagram_labels: Figure 1. Configuration in RHEONICS CONTROL PANEL to use Ethernet IP with DHCP.
Figure 2. Disable for DHCP, this will allow Static IP Address
2.3. For Static IP address go to the “Comm Configure tab” in the RHEONICS CONTROL PANEL; in the Ethernet section click the dropdown menu in DHCP and select false (This will disable DHCP function)
2.3.1. Fill all the parameters for the Ethernet/IP, remember to use valid values
warnings_notes: 2.3. For Static IP address... (This will disable DHCP function)

3. PLC Setup
This section explains how to navigate in the CCW software to configure the correct network settings. Rheonics provides a premade. CCWARC as a basic program for the Ethernet/ip connection.

3.1. RS Linx Classic
3.1.1. Once the software has been installed open the RSLinx Classic software.

Figure 4. Opening Rslinx Classic

3.1.2. Scan all systems in the network. To do this, click on “Rswho” button.

key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software (version 4.3.0.2) provides configuration options for Modbus RTU, Ethernet, Bluetooth LTE 4.0, LCD display parameters, location ID, and operation history.
The Modbus RTU (RS-485) settings are configured with an address of 1, a baud rate of 38400, and ODD parity.
The Ethernet connection is set with DHCP disabled, a static IP of 192.168.8.133, subnet 255.255.255.0, gateway IP 192.168.8.1, and DNS 192.168.8.1.
The Bluetooth LTE 4.0 MAC address is 70:B3:D5:D2:02:7B.
The LCD display is configured to show Viscosity, Density, and Temperature readings, each with a specified resolution.
Operational history shows an RTC Time of 1633019407, Supply Voltage of 23.70 V, Hardware Version 6, SME Temp of 34.90, Max. Sensor Temp. Exposure of 299.98, and Max. SME Temp. Exposure of 228.68.
The document instructs users to use CCW software and a premade CCWARC program for configuring Ethernet/IP network settings for PLC Setup.
RSLinx Classic software is utilized to scan and identify systems on the network, with an instruction to click the “Rswho” button for this purpose.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions and screenshots for scanning network devices using RSLinx Classic Lite to identify and prepare for the installation of an unrecognized Rheonics SRV sensor.
chunks.extracted_text:
3.1.3. Check for the following screen to be displayed:
Figure 5. Scan all network devices by pushing the” Rs Who” Button
3.1.4. Under the “AB_ETHIP-1, Ethernet” menu, the PLC and an unrecognized device with a yellow question mark will be found. (Figure 5. The latest in our SRV sensor, the .eds provided should be installed).
Figure 6. Devices found with the RSWho function
Figure 7. Unrecognized device for SRV electronic sensor
3.1.5.Follow the next steps to finish the .eds installation:
3.1.5.1. Open the EDS hardware Installation Tool
key_facts: The document describes how to use RSLinx Classic Lite to scan for network devices.
An unrecognized Rheonics SRV sensor, specifically model RH BJBW-B00-0000 SRV, will appear with a yellow question mark in the RSLinx Classic Lite browser under the 'AB_ETHIP-1, Ethernet' menu.
A PLC (Micro820, 2080-LC20-20QBB) is also found on the network.
The provided .eds (Electronic Data Sheet) file for the SRV sensor must be installed to properly recognize the device.
The next step in the .eds installation process is to open the EDS hardware Installation Tool.
warnings_notes: The latest .eds (Electronic Data Sheet) file provided for our SRV sensor should be installed for proper device recognition.

key_facts: The page describes a procedure for adding .eds files to a computer using the Rockwell Automation - Hardware Installation Tool (version 26.0.27.0).
The EDS Hardware Installation Tool manages hardware description information installed on the computer.
Users need to select the 'Add' button in the tool to launch the EDS Wizard and install Rheonics .eds files.
Specific Rheonics .eds files mentioned include RheonicsSRV.eds, RheonicsSRD.eds, RheonicsDVP.eds, and RheonicsDVM.eds.
The RSWho - 1 window shows network devices, including an 'Unrecognized Device' at IP 192.168.8.133 (RH BJBW-B00-0000 SRV) and a Micro820 at IP 192.168.8.134 (2080-LC20-20QBB).
diagram_labels: This tool allows you to change the hardware description information currently installed on your computer.
Launch the EDS Wizard and add selected hardware description files only
Launch the EDS Wizard and remove selected hardware description files only.

The RheonicsSRV.eds file was last modified on August 24, 2021, at 6:03 AM.
The RheonicsSRV.eds file has a size of 129 KB.
The wizard facilitates the registration of the SRV Viscometer device.
The registration process involves selecting the EDS file, reviewing installation test results, optionally changing the associated icon, and confirming the registration summary.

key_facts: The Rheonics SRV.eds file installation procedure ensures the sensor is recognized in RSLinx software.
After successful EDS installation, the RSLinx software should display the Rheonics icon for the recognized sensor, replacing any question mark.
The screenshot demonstrates an SRV Viscometer (RH BJBW-B00-0000 SRV) with IP address 192.168.8.133 detected in an Ethernet/IP network via RSLinx.
A Micro820 device (2080-LC20-20QBB) with IP address 192.168.8.134 is also visible in the RSLinx network browser.

Rheonics provides support articles on various topics including Ethernet connections on SMET, EthernetIP EDS files for viscometers and density meters, parameter list access over EthernetIP, and Ethernet/IP integration with Micro820-Connected Components Workbench.
